summary: Migrate authentication from session cookies to JWT across three services. Touches every user, every service, live session state, and the compliance surface. Textbook full-rigor, irreversible-in-flight migration.

reversibility.why: Mid-migration rollback is painful: tokens are issued, sessions invalidated, clients cached; partial rollback strands users.

blast_radius.why: Every authenticated request across three services goes through the new path; a bug logs everyone out.

compliance_scope.why: Authentication is a SOC2 control and usually in PCI/HIPAA audit scope; auth rewrites are always compliance events.

user_facing_impact.why: Every user experiences the cutover; any session-loss manifests as a forced logout.

novelty.why: JWT migration patterns are well-known, but this codebase hasn't done the specific three-service dance before.

scope_effort.why: Three services, libraries, client-side code, and operational tooling all change.

state_complexity.why: Live session store must co-exist with token issuance during the cutover; rotation + revocation semantics are stateful.

operational_risk.why: Any hiccup in token validation is an immediate P1; rollback drill must be exercised before cutover.

coordination_cost.why: Security, SRE, three service teams, release, and compliance all must sign off.

specialists.name: requirements-analyst
specialists.why: Capture formal requirements and REQ- IDs for auditability.

specialists.name: product-manager
specialists.why: Own user-facing narrative, rollout comms, and cutover windows.

specialists.name: solution-architect
specialists.why: Design the dual-stack period, key rotation, and service-to-service trust model.

specialists.name: senior-engineer
specialists.why: Technical lead across services; arbitrate contract-level design decisions.

specialists.name: backend-engineer
specialists.why: Implement issuance, validation, and middleware in each service.

specialists.name: security-engineer
specialists.why: Threat-model the JWT flow, algorithm choice, key management, and replay/invalidation controls.

specialists.name: compliance-officer
specialists.why: Attest SOC2 / applicable regulatory controls and produce traceability artifacts.

specialists.name: migration-engineer
specialists.why: Own the dual-stack cutover plan, session-to-token bridge, and rollback procedure.

specialists.name: sre
specialists.why: Observability, SLOs, rollback drill, and on-call readiness for the cutover.

specialists.name: release-engineer
specialists.why: Sequence the staged rollout across three services and clients.

specialists.name: test-strategist
specialists.why: Shape the full pyramid including security and performance coverage.

specialists.name: test-designer
specialists.why: Author the integration, api, security, and acceptance suites.

specialists.name: independent-reviewer
specialists.why: Multi-reviewer structure required for full-rigor irreversible work.

phases.name: clarify
phases.why: Formal requirements, cutover constraints, compliance envelope pinned down.

phases.name: design
phases.why: Architecture + security + migration design, including `/wicked-garden:jam:council` multi-model review for irreversible choices.

phases.name: test-strategy
phases.why: Plan full pyramid with security and performance explicitly called out.

phases.name: build
phases.why: Implement issuance, validation, and middleware per service with dual-stack support.

phases.name: review
phases.why: Independent reviewer + compliance officer sign-off before cutover.

rigor_why: Auth rewrite crosses compliance, reversibility, blast radius, operational risk, and coordination — every full-rigor amplifier fires simultaneously.
complexity_why: Cross-service, stateful, compliance-bound migration with coordinated cutover — high end of the scale, short of a green-field rewrite (7).
open_questions: Algorithm and key-rotation policy: RS256 with JWKS and 24h rotation, or tenant-scoped HS256?
Dual-stack window length and rollback trigger thresholds?
yolo_refusal_reason: rigor_tier=full — per the rubric, auto_proceed is ignored for full-rigor work. This change is irreversible in mid-flight and carries SOC2/PCI implications; user confirmation and independent review are mandatory.

tasks.title: Capture REQ- IDs, compliance envelope, and cutover constraints
Architecture + security + migration design (with council review)
Full-pyramid test strategy including security and performance
Implement JWT issuance + validation across 3 services (dual-stack)
Security threat model + scan of new auth surface
Migration cutover plan + rehearsal + rollback drill
Compliance traceability package (REQ → design → code → test)
